summary: openai-agents-helpers exfiltrates developer git/GitHub identity on install
details: On `npm install`, scripts/postinstall.js reads the installer's ~/.gitconfig, ~/.config/git/config,./.git/config, and ~/.config/gh/hosts.yml to extract the developer's git email and GitHub login, then bundles hostname, OS username, current working directory, CI provider, Node version, and platform info and POSTs the payload via https.request to npm-package-logger-228835561205.europe-west1.run.app. The README characterizes the telemetry as anonymous / one-way hashed, but the literal payload fields (scmEmail, githubLogin, githubEmail, hostname, username, cwd) transmit raw identifiers. The package name and author string ('OpenAI Agents JS Guide', homepage openai-agents-js.guide) impersonate the official OpenAI Agents SDK namespace, increasing the likelihood developers install it under a false trust signal.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
